TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_baeket, title = baeket, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/baeket} }

S.FORM: == baeket == ťáć,ś≤Čťáć / baeket kina mamukuen a ba'tu. u zuma ku baeket nu nizateng ni Kacaw. siwkay nu baeket u baeket sanay, namatengteng ku Kitakit. u canacanan a tuutuud, pabahekul i pabaw, amahetik i lala' tu. u baetu sa baeket, u kazizeng sa baeket tu. u kizmu a tademaw baeket, u makudusay a tademaw sa caay kabaeket. hacica ku baeket sa, si sakaluk, u daduy. tinaku a kamu baeketay ťáćÁöĄ / baeketay azihen kiya nimuku ni Ubad a mukin. baeketen Ť¶ļŚĺóŚ§™ťáć / baeketen kaku tu daduy nu maku. pabaeketen Śä†ťáć / pabaeketen ku nimuku nu tatama tu pawti nu tipus! sabaeket Śĺąťáć / sabaeket sa ku niculil ni Benel. sabaeketay śúÄťáćÁöĄ / sabaeketay ku ni katabaki nu uzip ni Pasang. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika In science and engineering, the weight of an object is usually taken to be the force on the object due to gravity.Weight is a vector whose magnitude , often denoted by an italic letter W, is the product of the mass m of the object and the magnitude of the local gravitational acceleration g; thus: W = mg. The unit of measurement for weight is that of force, which in the International System of Units is the newton. For example, an object with a mass of one kilogram has a weight of about 9.8 newtons on the surface of the Earth, and about one-sixth as much on the Moon. In this sense of weight, a body can be weightless only if it is distant from any other mass. Although weight and mass are scientifically distinct quantities, the terms are often confused with each other in everyday use (i.e. comparing and converting force weight in pounds to mass in kilograms and vice versa).
S.PHON: == ba…ôk…ôt == *,** / ba…ôk…ôt kina mamuku…ôn a ba Ē~ °tu. u zuma ku ba…ôk…ôt nu nizat…ôŇč ni ka ¶aw. siwkaj nu ba…ôk…ôt u ba…ôk…ôt sanaj, namat…ôŇčt…ôŇč ku kitakit. u ¶ana ¶anan a tuutuud, pabańß…ôkur~…ĺ i pabaw, amańß…ôtik i r~…ĺar~…ĺa Ē~ ° tu. u ba…ôtu sa ba…ôk…ôt, u kaziz…ôŇč sa ba…ôk…ôt tu. u kizmu a tad…ômaw ba…ôk…ôt, u makudusaj a tad…ômaw sa ¶aaj kaba…ôk…ôt. ńßa ¶i ¶a ku ba…ôk…ôt sa, si sakar~…ĺuk, u daduj. tinaku a kamu ba…ôk…ôtaj ** / ba…ôk…ôtaj azińß…ôn kija nimuku ni ubad a mukin. ba…ôk…ôt…ôn **** / ba…ôk…ôt…ôn kaku tu daduj nu maku. paba…ôk…ôt…ôn ** / paba…ôk…ôt…ôn ku nimuku nu tatama tu pawti nu tipus! saba…ôk…ôt ** / saba…ôk…ôt sa ku ni ¶ur~…ĺir~…ĺ ni b…ôn…ôr~…ĺ. saba…ôk…ôtaj *** / saba…ôk…ôtaj ku ni katabaki nu uzip ni pasaŇč. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika in s ¶i…ôn ¶…ô and …ôŇčin…ô…ôriŇč, tńß…ô w…ôigńßt of an obj…ô ¶t is usuar~…ĺr~…ĺj tak…ôn to b…ô tńß…ô for ¶…ô on tńß…ô obj…ô ¶t du…ô to gra*itj.w…ôigńßt is a *…ô ¶tor wńßos…ô magnitud…ô , oft…ôn d…ônot…ôd bj an itar~…ĺi ¶ r~…ĺ…ôtt…ôr w, is tńß…ô produ ¶t of tńß…ô mass m of tńß…ô obj…ô ¶t and tńß…ô magnitud…ô of tńß…ô r~…ĺo ¶ar~…ĺ gra*itationar~…ĺ a ¶ ¶…ôr~…ĺ…ôration g; tńßus: w = mg. tńß…ô unit of m…ôasur…ôm…ônt for w…ôigńßt is tńßat of for ¶…ô, wńßi ¶ńß in tńß…ô int…ôrnationar~…ĺ sjst…ôm of units is tńß…ô n…ôwton. for …ô*ampr~…ĺ…ô, an obj…ô ¶t witńß a mass of on…ô kir~…ĺogram ńßas a w…ôigńßt of about *.* n…ôwtons on tńß…ô surfa ¶…ô of tńß…ô …ôartńß, and about on…ô-si*tńß as mu ¶ńß on tńß…ô moon. in tńßis s…ôns…ô of w…ôigńßt, a bodj ¶an b…ô w…ôigńßtr~…ĺ…ôss onr~…ĺj if it is distant from anj otńß…ôr mass. ar~…ĺtńßougńß w…ôigńßt and mass ar…ô s ¶i…ôntifi ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺj distin ¶t *uantiti…ôs, tńß…ô t…ôrms ar…ô oft…ôn ¶onfus…ôd witńß …ôa ¶ńß otńß…ôr in …ô*…ôrjdaj us…ô (i.…ô. ¶ompariŇč and ¶on*…ôrtiŇč for ¶…ô w…ôigńßt in pounds to mass in kir~…ĺograms and *i ¶…ô *…ôrsa).
S.FORM: == baeket == ťáć,ś≤Čťáć / baeket kina mamukuen a ba'tu. u zuma ku baeket nu nizateng ni Kacaw. siwkay nu baeket u baeket sanay, namatengteng ku Kitakit. u canacanan a tuutuud, pabahekul i pabaw, amahetik i lala' tu. u baetu sa baeket, u kazizeng sa baeket tu. u kizmu a tademaw baeket, u makudusay a tademaw sa caay kabaeket. hacica ku baeket sa, si sakaluk, u daduy. tinaku a kamu baeketay ťáćÁöĄ / baeketay azihen kiya nimuku ni Ubad a mukin. baeketen Ť¶ļŚĺóŚ§™ťáć / baeketen kaku tu daduy nu maku. pabaeketen Śä†ťáć / pabaeketen ku nimuku nu tatama tu pawti nu tipus! sabaeket Śĺąťáć / sabaeket sa ku niculil ni Benel. sabaeketay śúÄťáćÁöĄ / sabaeketay ku ni katabaki nu uzip ni Pasang. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika In science and engineering, the weight of an object is usually taken to be the force on the object due to gravity.Weight is a vector whose magnitude , often denoted by an italic letter W, is the product of the mass m of the object and the magnitude of the local gravitational acceleration g; thus: W = mg. The unit of measurement for weight is that of force, which in the International System of Units is the newton. For example, an object with a mass of one kilogram has a weight of about 9.8 newtons on the surface of the Earth, and about one-sixth as much on the Moon. In this sense of weight, a body can be weightless only if it is distant from any other mass. Although weight and mass are scientifically distinct quantities, the terms are often confused with each other in everyday use (i.e. comparing and converting force weight in pounds to mass in kilograms and vice versa).
S.PHON: == ba…ôk…ôt == *,** / ba…ôk…ôt kina mamuku…ôn a ba Ē~ °tu. u zuma ku ba…ôk…ôt nu nizat…ôŇč ni ka ¶aw. siwkaj nu ba…ôk…ôt u ba…ôk…ôt sanaj, namat…ôŇčt…ôŇč ku kitakit. u ¶ana ¶anan a tuutuud, pabańß…ôkur~…ĺ i pabaw, amańß…ôtik i r~…ĺar~…ĺa Ē~ ° tu. u ba…ôtu sa ba…ôk…ôt, u kaziz…ôŇč sa ba…ôk…ôt tu. u kizmu a tad…ômaw ba…ôk…ôt, u makudusaj a tad…ômaw sa ¶aaj kaba…ôk…ôt. ńßa ¶i ¶a ku ba…ôk…ôt sa, si sakar~…ĺuk, u daduj. tinaku a kamu ba…ôk…ôtaj ** / ba…ôk…ôtaj azińß…ôn kija nimuku ni ubad a mukin. ba…ôk…ôt…ôn **** / ba…ôk…ôt…ôn kaku tu daduj nu maku. paba…ôk…ôt…ôn ** / paba…ôk…ôt…ôn ku nimuku nu tatama tu pawti nu tipus! saba…ôk…ôt ** / saba…ôk…ôt sa ku ni ¶ur~…ĺir~…ĺ ni b…ôn…ôr~…ĺ. saba…ôk…ôtaj *** / saba…ôk…ôtaj ku ni katabaki nu uzip ni pasaŇč. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika in s ¶i…ôn ¶…ô and …ôŇčin…ô…ôriŇč, tńß…ô w…ôigńßt of an obj…ô ¶t is usuar~…ĺr~…ĺj tak…ôn to b…ô tńß…ô for ¶…ô on tńß…ô obj…ô ¶t du…ô to gra*itj.w…ôigńßt is a *…ô ¶tor wńßos…ô magnitud…ô , oft…ôn d…ônot…ôd bj an itar~…ĺi ¶ r~…ĺ…ôtt…ôr w, is tńß…ô produ ¶t of tńß…ô mass m of tńß…ô obj…ô ¶t and tńß…ô magnitud…ô of tńß…ô r~…ĺo ¶ar~…ĺ gra*itationar~…ĺ a ¶ ¶…ôr~…ĺ…ôration g; tńßus: w = mg. tńß…ô unit of m…ôasur…ôm…ônt for w…ôigńßt is tńßat of for ¶…ô, wńßi ¶ńß in tńß…ô int…ôrnationar~…ĺ sjst…ôm of units is tńß…ô n…ôwton. for …ô*ampr~…ĺ…ô, an obj…ô ¶t witńß a mass of on…ô kir~…ĺogram ńßas a w…ôigńßt of about *.* n…ôwtons on tńß…ô surfa ¶…ô of tńß…ô …ôartńß, and about on…ô-si*tńß as mu ¶ńß on tńß…ô moon. in tńßis s…ôns…ô of w…ôigńßt, a bodj ¶an b…ô w…ôigńßtr~…ĺ…ôss onr~…ĺj if it is distant from anj otńß…ôr mass. ar~…ĺtńßougńß w…ôigńßt and mass ar…ô s ¶i…ôntifi ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺj distin ¶t *uantiti…ôs, tńß…ô t…ôrms ar…ô oft…ôn ¶onfus…ôd witńß …ôa ¶ńß otńß…ôr in …ô*…ôrjdaj us…ô (i.…ô. ¶ompariŇč and ¶on*…ôrtiŇč for ¶…ô w…ôigńßt in pounds to mass in kir~…ĺograms and *i ¶…ô *…ôrsa).
